pi_items.source: OpenBCI Documentation: OpenBCI Cyton boards record at 250 samples per second. Confirmed via official docs at https://docs.openbci.com/Software/OpenBCISoftware/GUIDocs/

Task description and verifier logic (export_result.sh and verifier.py). Standard EEG baseline recording duration. Calculation: 5 sec × 250 Hz = 1250 samples + ~5-6 header lines ≈ 1255-1256 total lines, supporting min_rows threshold of 1000.

Derived from sample_rate (250 Hz) and min_duration_seconds (5 sec). Expected total line count: 5 × 250 = 1250 data rows + ~5-6 header comment lines = ~1255-1256 total lines. Metadata threshold of 1000 is conservative and consistent.
Verified in setup_task.sh line 30 and export_result.sh line 14. Filename is explicitly set and matches across all scripts.
Verified in setup_task.sh line 13 and export_result.sh line 13. Directory path is consistent across all setup and export scripts.
Verified in setup_task.sh line 14. Filename follows OpenBCI naming convention for playback files. 'S001' indicates Subject 001, 'EyesOpen' indicates baseline condition used for EEG calibration.

pi_items.verified_value: Real EEG data (S001 baseline, Eyes Open condition)
pi_items.source: Task uses real EEG playback file from subject S001 in eyes-open state (standard baseline condition in EEG research). Not synthetic/algorithmic data. Confirmed by task description and OpenBCI standard practices documented at https://docs.openbci.com/GettingStarted/Biosensing-Setups/EEGSetup/

pi_items.verified_value: OpenBCI-RAW CSV text format with comment headers
pi_items.source: Verified in export_result.sh lines 37-39 (format check looks for '%' and ',' characters). OpenBCI documentation confirms format is CSV with percent-prefixed comment lines for headers.

privileged_info_summary: This task records EEG data using OpenBCI GUI with a playback file from Subject 001 in eyes-open baseline state. The recording is captured at the standard OpenBCI Cyton sample rate of 250 Hz. The minimum recording duration is 5 seconds, which yields approximately 1250-1256 total lines (including ~5-6 header lines), supporting the metadata threshold of min_rows: 1000. The target file is renamed to 'alpha_calibration.txt' in ~/Documents/OpenBCI_GUI/Recordings/. The underlying EEG data is real, not synthetic.
